So I showed you I did that one brochure once one year and the rest of the time what I've always used is my stuff All right, so client inquiry gets to a certain point. We take all the books And we like post-it notes certain sections of them that are relevant to that guy we get some CDs We gave me pile of testimonial letters We put it on a big box and we sent it to them Well, nobody sends them all that crap. We send bobbleheads You know who there who sends them a bobblehead of the guy on a bull nobody sends them that right? So and they don't see nobody's gonna throw that out You know, they're gonna I don't know what they're do with them, but they ain't chucking them I mean I've had people tell me, you know, they couldn't find what to do with it. So Their kids got it they got it on the shelf in their office and people ask them about it And they they don't know why they got it on the shelf in the office, but they just don't feel like they should throw it out Well, that's perfect Right. I've sent clocks I've sent Now for me, it's more about media than it is so I can book promotion. So, you know, like we've got any I Got an interview coming up In a magazine and I started sending that guy stuff a year ago Never really asked for nothing. I just sent him stuff And more stuff and more stuff and more stuff and now once the interview is booked I've sent him my no BS time management wall clock. I've sent him to bobbleheads. So he's got one for his kid I sent him one of every book with post-adodated sections. He should read. I'm dripping on him between now and the interview Here's what everybody else does. They sent him a press kit two pockets Some stuff stuffed in each pocket a little business card stuffed in the four corners and he gets like 20 of those a week That's what he gets and they're all interchangeable So if you're gonna cold prospect and you're gonna send people stuff you better really wow them with the stuff right and better It not all be pretty Because pretty much what everybody sends them is pretty Depending on the market some of it has to be pretty but it doesn't all have to be pretty and amongst the stuff The best thing you can send of course is a book or books because Nothing has more Assumed expert status than you wrote a book And they don't have to be I brought some some of them are embarrassing actually, but if you go back far enough And you can take a look at this stuff out of breaks if you want to here's a Well first of all here's a copywriter guy John Francis Tige I may be mispronouncing him name on Miss Browns for years He's got that quote that I really like in the country of the blind the one I meant man is king and he wears an eyepatch Your understanding points of the quote by the way to consultants and coaches so you don't have to know everything You just have to know more than they do right But and this is a but this is a good book You see I got it posted noted and highlighted and stuff. I mean it's a good book, but really it's a promotion It's his calling card. It's a promotional book and it's self-published Which he could have done a little better job of making that look more real If you had thought about it, but there ain't much to it. It's I don't know. It's 80 some odd pages The chapters are short The pages the chapter start all the way down on the page, you know, I Mean he pretty much wrote I think it's probably the fewest number of pages you can bind So I mean he pretty much did as little as he possibly could do To be able to have a book instead of a brochure Right and he's done very very very well Has a copywriter with this tool way back This is really a crappy little thing, but it made us both a lot of money Bill Brooks and I did one together Say money two of us to the same book and I don't want to have much money On time management and there ain't much to it if you look at it like we covered whole pages with graphics Right less gotta do graphics Chart charts are good Here's a whole chapter You
